title: Fix portal sections not rendering after Version 36 deployment

requirements.summary: Diagnose and fix the portal layout so that all sections (Memberships, Publishing Works, Releases, Recording Projects, Artist Development) render correctly for authenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: All five portal section pages (Memberships, Publishing Works, Releases, Recording Projects, Artist Development) are visible and navigable from the sidebar in PortalLayout.
No blank or empty portal shell is displayed after a successful login.
The PortalLayout sidebar navigation items render correctly for both admin and non-admin authenticated users.
Child routes within the portal render their list/content views without console errors.
The portal loads consistently on page refresh without losing section visibility.

file_operations.description: Audit and verify the PortalLayout component renders the sidebar navigation and Outlet correctly. Check for missing Outlet component, conditional rendering bugs, or state issues that could cause the main content area to be blank. Ensure the sidebar navigation links are present and the mobile drawer toggle functions without breaking layout. Add defensive error boundaries if needed to prevent total layout failure.

Verify the TanStack Router route tree includes all portal child routes (memberships, publishing, releases, recordings, artist development, admin) correctly nested under the portal parent route. Check that AuthGate wrapper logic is not incorrectly redirecting authenticated users. Ensure ApprovalGate remains disabled (rendering children unconditionally). Confirm Outlet components are present at each nesting level. Add console logging or debugging aids to trace route resolution if needed.

Verify the MembershipsPage component renders without runtime errors. Check for missing imports, hooks returning undefined, or conditional rendering that could cause a blank page. Ensure data fetching hooks (useGetAllMembershipProfiles, useGetCallerMemberships) handle loading/error states correctly. Confirm the component returns valid JSX in all code paths.

Verify the PublishingWorksPage component renders without runtime errors. Check for missing imports, hooks returning undefined, or conditional rendering that could cause a blank page. Ensure data fetching hooks (useGetAllPublishingWorks) handle loading/error states correctly. Confirm the component returns valid JSX in all code paths.

Verify the ReleasesPage component renders without runtime errors. Check for missing imports, hooks returning undefined, or conditional rendering that could cause a blank page. Ensure data fetching hooks (useGetAllReleases) handle loading/error states correctly. Confirm the component returns valid JSX in all code paths.

Verify the RecordingProjectsPage component renders without runtime errors. Check for missing imports, hooks returning undefined, or conditional rendering that could cause a blank page. Ensure data fetching hooks (useGetAllRecordingProjects) handle loading/error states correctly. Confirm the component returns valid JSX in all code paths.

Verify the ArtistDevelopmentPage component renders without runtime errors. Check for missing imports, hooks returning undefined, or conditional rendering that could cause a blank page. Ensure data fetching hooks (useGetAllArtistDevelopment) handle loading/error states correctly. Confirm the component returns valid JSX in all code paths.

requirements.summary: Audit the TanStack Router route tree in App.tsx to ensure all portal child routes are correctly defined, nested under the portal parent route, and not inadvertently blocked by AuthGate or ApprovalGate wrappers
acceptanceCriteria: All portal sub-routes (memberships, publishing, releases, recordings, artist development, admin) are present and correctly nested in the route tree.
AuthGate does not incorrectly redirect authenticated users away from portal routes.
ApprovalGate (currently disabled) does not block rendering of portal sections.
Navigating directly to a portal sub-route URL renders the correct page content.

file_operations.description: Conduct a comprehensive audit of the route tree structure. Verify every portal child route (memberships, publishing, releases, recordings, artist development, admin) is defined and properly nested. Check AuthGate configuration to ensure it allows authenticated users through. Verify ApprovalGate remains disabled. Test each route's path property for correctness. Ensure the portal parent route has an Outlet component. Add route debugging or logging if needed to trace resolution issues.

Review the AuthGate logic to ensure it does not incorrectly redirect authenticated users. Verify the condition that checks authentication status is correct and does not have false positives. Check for race conditions or timing issues where identity might not be ready when the gate evaluates. Ensure the redirect target is correct and does not create redirect loops.

Confirm the ApprovalGate component is disabled and always renders children unconditionally. Verify no conditional logic was accidentally re-enabled in Version 36. Ensure the disabled state is clearly commented and will not be inadvertently reverted.

requirements.summary: Verify and fix the PortalLayout component to ensure the sidebar navigation links and the main content outlet are rendering. Confirm that the router outlet (Outlet) is present and active so that child route components are actually mounted and displayed
acceptanceCriteria: PortalLayout renders its sidebar with all navigation links.
The main content area displays the active child route component.
No runtime errors are thrown from PortalLayout that would cause the entire portal shell to go blank.
Mobile drawer toggle functions correctly without breaking the layout.

file_operations.description: Perform a detailed review of PortalLayout structure. Verify the Outlet component from TanStack Router is imported and rendered in the main content area. Check that sidebar navigation items are correctly mapped and rendered. Inspect conditional rendering logic for admin items to ensure non-admin users still see the base navigation. Test mobile drawer state management for bugs. Add defensive null checks and error boundaries to prevent total layout failure. Verify CSS classes are not hiding content unintentionally.

Review the useCurrentUser hook to ensure it returns data in the expected format for PortalLayout's conditional admin navigation rendering. Check for undefined or null values that could break conditional logic. Verify loading states are handled correctly to prevent premature rendering decisions. Ensure the hook does not throw errors that would crash PortalLayout.
